source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A building is made up of cubes of the same size. The three pictures show it from above (von oben), from the front (von vorne) and from the right (von rechts). What is the maximum number of cubes used to make this building?
<image1>

image_dependency: Question explicitly refers to three pictures/views: above, front, and right
Solving depends on interpreting spatial information from the images rather than text alone
asset_registry_record.image_manifest shows 1 available image and registry_passed=true
multi_step: Need to combine constraints from three orthographic views (top/front/right)
Question asks for the maximum number of cubes, implying reconstruction under multiple constraints rather than a single lookup
This involves spatial reasoning and optimization over consistent cube arrangements
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single number: 19
A candidate construction can be checked against the three stated views
asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true, so the core assets needed for checking are available

decision_reasons: Core assets are complete: question, image, and numeric answer are all present, and registry_passed=true.
The sample has strong image dependency and multi-step spatial reasoning value based on the three-view cube reconstruction setup.
Verifiability is high because the answer is a single number and a proposed construction can be checked against the views.
The only noted risk is mixed_language_question, but this does not prevent recovery of the core semantic content.
